title: Using rubrics and inline feedback in Assignments
what_it_is: A rubric is a built-in marking grid attached to a Teams Assignment, listing the criteria and points a piece of work is marked against. Inline feedback is a comment or annotation added directly onto a learner's submitted document, next to the part it refers to.
why_use_it: A rubric makes marking criteria clear and consistent for every learner, and inline feedback lets a learner see exactly which part of their work a comment relates to, rather than a general note at the end.
how_to: Open the Assignment in Teams, either while creating it or from an existing one.
Select Add rubric, then choose New rubric to build one from scratch, or Reuse rubric to use one you've created before.
Add each criterion you're marking against, along with the points or rating levels for it, then select Attach.
Once learners have submitted their work, open a submission from the Assignment's grading view.
Use the rubric on the right-hand side to select the points or level for each criterion — this fills in the overall grade automatically.
To leave inline feedback, select the part of the document you want to comment on, then select New Comment and type your feedback.
Once marking is complete, select Return to send the grade and feedback back to the learner.
